subject: Geology
title: Original Horizontality
description: A short lesson to help you apply Original Horizontality.

hook.text: Sediment settles flat. If you find tilted rock layers, something happened after they formed. But what?

buildup.text: The principle of original horizontality says sediment layers are deposited in roughly horizontal sheets.

discovery.text: Tilted, folded, or vertical layers tell us that tectonic forces acted after the sediment was laid down.

twist.text: This simple idea is incredibly powerful. It turns deformed rock into evidence of past earthquakes and collisions.

climax.text: Every tilted layer is a clue. Original horizontality tells us what the starting position looked like.

punchline.text: If the layers aren't flat, forces moved them.

quiz.question: What does original horizontality tell us?
options: Sediment layers start horizontal; tilting means later forces acted
All rock layers are the same age
